title: IREN's AI Cloud Pivot Backed by $5.8B Investment Targets $3.7B Revenue
text: IREN (NASDAQ: IREN) is transforming from Bitcoin mining into AI cloud services. The company aims to generate $3.7 billion in annual revenue through its new business model, supported by a substantial $5.8 billion investment in GPU infrastructure. IREN has secured a five-year agreement with Microsoft for AI workloads, which includes customer prepayments to fund expansion efforts and reduce reliance on expensive borrowing. The company plans to deploy 150,000 GPUs by the end of 2026, repurposing existing Bitcoin mining facilities in Texas and British Columbia with cutting-edge AI computing equipment. Bernstein analysts assigned IREN a $100 price target, representing nearly 100% upside from its April 2026 trading level of $50.64, though this reflects a reduction from their earlier $125 target due to equity dilution concerns. This pivot mirrors broader industry trends as Bitcoin miners face declining profitability from rising energy costs and regulatory pressures. IREN's existing infrastructure including abundant low-cost renewable energy, robust cooling systems, and established facilities provides competitive advantages for GPU-intensive AI operations. The shift positions IREN to establish itself as a key player in the rapidly expanding AI infrastructure market while reducing exposure to cryptocurrency market volatility.
